channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 117 replies — 468.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:58 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 91 replies — 364.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:55 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451742
>>62451539
People who have worked their whole lives at least have learned HOW to work and have a resume to prove it. But that is really only the bare minimum. There is much more to a fulfilling life, much of which has nothing to do with work or money, but still arises out of self-discipline, skill-development, and emotionally maturity.

62451745
>>62451705
Dentalfag reporting in.
If you post some pics I can tell you which ones could be saved with a crown and which you should have extracted ASAP

62451748
>>62451705
Forgot to add you need to fix your teeth by any means necessary, even if it means ripping that shit out of your head because if you don't it will kill you slowly and painfully by infection. Just aggressively pursue free state services.

62451816
I like these threads, it makes me feel better about my own life. But OP seriously how did you get to this point? You would think alarm bells would be going off in your head at age 25, 30 etc. that you need to get your shit together, for fuck sakes your parents aren't going to live forever.

62451821
>>62451099
It's thievery
He can't admit to crimes nigga
You dense

62451832
>>62451403
Wow shouldn't have cut internet mom lol

62451837
Healthcare or zogbot
Neither a comfy choice, but assuming healthcare, you can start sweeping floors then move up to some sort of auxiliary nurse, nursing assistant, then nurse eventually. You could reach 6 figs, but the path would take a fair amount of humiliation, wiping floors and boomer feces

62451874
>>62451745
This anon is offering something genuinely good for seemingly free.
I'm just noting that

62452257
>>62451063
OP check out this guy sno0ks on YouTube, he has just gone through a similar life experience to you and has vlogged about it: https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=VQsdy_yaQAQ&ra=m

62452288
Go to school
Get work experience
Get a real job
Jesus Christ most people figure this out when they’re 18 years old you god damn retard.

62452842
>>62451745
There's nothing to save, I have two teeth that are broken off at the gum line and the gums have grown over them, another tooth lost the back half above the gum line and my lower right molar is broken on top and has a chunk missing.
They all have to come out.

62452847
>>62452288
What school? I'm already a high school graduate.
>>62451821
Food stamps, dum dum.

62453356
What about trying to start a business? Self employment? Why do my only options have to be depressing bullshit?

62453476
>>62453356

62453498
>>62453356
Both are better (best?) options for wealth if you don't plan on going to college and also marrying someone who went to school. Both preferably masters degrees or higher.

62453510
>>62449961
I have an online friend who was a loser NEET for like 10 years, he's still a loser but he started editing videos for youtube channels for a % of revenue and one of them popped off and he's made around 220k in 2 years. If you are autistic about something and use the internet, it's possible, though you have to get lucky and work hard still

62453537
>>62453356
You need some capital to get started, whatever biz you want to open. And since you sound incredibly lazy and your body most likely haven't a single gram of muscular mass, you probably wont do anything outside, correct?
Then you need to make cash from your computer.

I started scalping and flipping TCGs back in 2016 with $4K invested. Slow start but it eventually paid off after a bunch of smart picks on my end (no sealed, i focused on singles at release i thought could become staples, extremely risky and you need to be very good at the game itself to correctly identify unnoticed bangers). Once i secured more cash i could start to focus on vintage staples and that's where the $$ are. Cashed out 100K total so it turned out pretty nicely.

If you have zero you can try to make AI pornslop, twitch content or stuff like that. But starting from scratches, with zero visibility, zero social skill and no determination in a field ultra-saturated with mega grinders who want to succeed... i don't think you'll go very far. Not to discourage you, but what you have showed us of your personality so far tells me you most likely will end up as a beggar living under a bridge.

62453557
>>62453510
Video editing is a shitload of work hours, if you reads OP shitpost you'll quickly understand it's out of his reach. He's basically asking if we can give him a winning lottery ticket, and when we tell him it doesn't work that way, he whines.

62453686
>>62453557
You would need a computer for that and an Internet connection. The desktop unused to have died several years ago and I'm only allowed to use the wifi with my phone.
I would have to move out completely first.

62453688
Also, if you guys could calm down on the personal attacks? I didn't do anything to deserve that shit. Whatever boggeyman lives in your head isn't me.

62453723
God, it feels like I'm having panic attack after panic attack.

62453813
>>62453723
honest to god you should go work on a boat/ship, get paid to roam the seas and hear stuff on the radio and sleep in the middle of the darkness of the ocean and occasionally see whales.

62453981
>>62449961
This is why I tell my neet friend to grt out and go grab a job, even packing groceries before you reach 40 something. YOU WILL REGRET NEETING

62454510
seconding personal home aide/caregiver
gotta get your shoe in the door somehow

https://www.uscareerinstitute.edu/online-caregiver-school
tuition: $485
2-4 months online part time training

62454560
Become a trans prostitute

62455526
I guess I need to see a dentist soon. That's next to do, I guess

62455565
Oh, is this the bump limit? Threads about to slide off the board?
>>62454560
You're insane or a glowie.

62455632
>>62454560
Can always start with trans feet pics. Get them all pedicured and female-passing first of course.

62455644
Are you artistically inclined?
No shit, you may be able to draw furry porn. Those guys still pay out the ass for commissions and even though AI is a thing a lot of furries refuse to use it to get their porn.

62455814
>>62449961
Good call making this on a Monday so you get the extra seethe from wagies that had to go back to work.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 85 replies — 340.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:59 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453347
>>62453303
You have 20 posts seething about indians who are the richest immigrants in America averaging $200k per year, yes you are jealous of us

62453353
>>62453347
>richest immigrants in America averaging $200k per year
Brapmeet thinks we don't know about his household income stats with ten jeets crammed into each domicile. KEK

62453370
>>62453353
Ten indian Google coders at $400,000 per year each stuffed in one house, in 5 years each one buys a $1 million house for cash.

While you seethe and cry on incel blogs that you deserve everything for free because you are white, lmao no wonder you are bitter

62453683
>>62449441
>Lives in a white country instead of his shithole
Go home Rajesh nobody likes you

62453692
>>62453683
Your master invited me in because you are dumb and lazy and im smart and hardworking

62453742
>>62453062
This is the most blatant
>Thing: Japan
I've ever seen in my life. As if all of rural north america doesn't do this as well.

62454011
Jugaad

62454019
I ended up canceling my membership because the Indian situation became intolerable to deal with. They refunded me my full $65. Thank you CostCo

62454027
>>62453353
Clown house.

62454038
>>62449441
Bloody bitch benchod!
Take it back!
I place a curse on your tribe, bitch!

62454126
>>62454038
SAAAAAAAARS

62454135
>>62454019
Indians have conquered your country and colonized your mind

62454143
>>62449769
Go back now or in a few years you will graduate to compost fertilizer. Not that you jeets can read the times.

62454145
>>62454019
costco lives or dies off its public relations, which is partly why they allow the kind of subhumans like OP's subject to carry on for a while before cancelling their membership to ensure they're not perceived poorly
But yeah, they're going to have to do something about the flood of shitskins using 10 incomes to a household to shop there and I don't think a dozen new locations each year will solve it

62454349
Is anyone shorting

62454398
>>62454145
Kek

62454476
just wait, in 2 more years costco will have a jeet ceo and instead of hotdogs youre gonna get currywurst

62454493
>>62454349
The richest cohort in America shopping there and white incels are shorting? Kektop!

62454541
>>62454493
Seethe

62454717
>>62451853
>Turns every one of those companies into scamming pieces of shit
What exactly are you proud of, you stupid shit eating jeet?

62455561
>>62454717
Kek

62455654
>>62454717
Beating the white incel losers at the game of life and hearing their cries bbbbbbut im white

62455686
>>62453347
>us
Your skin is the same color as poo

62455702
>>62455654
>t.

62455754
>>62454135

62455759
>>62455686
You cant breed, kektop

62455766
>>62453370

62455771
>>62455759
Post stack
But you won't because you're poor and you'll always be poor.

62455822
>>62455771
Rocks? Lmao what a loser

62455829
>>62455822
Sour grapes

item.title: Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/? — 81 replies — 291.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:43 UTC

OP

Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Early replies (first 3)

62447635
>>62447595
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sZZaOR4UCnE

62447641
>>62447595
The only smart play is to aim for a 100% disability rating which you can get in ~1 to 4 years (1 enlistment).
I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life.
I am a vet and I get nothing.

62447664
>>62447595
If you're not high rank or close to information in some other way then it's just another job. Not gonna change much in terms of how getting a million would look like, you'll have to gamble like the rest of us.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451215
>>62448579
>>62450947
youre so mindbroken lmao

62451282
>>62451215
You will never be a patriot

62451292
>>62447641
This. From everything I've heard and seen, aiming for disability is the way to go.

You will probably lose your dignity in the pursuit of this though, but the long-term benefits are probably worth it. They might be clamping down on this under Hegseth I imagine, but maybe it depends on your branch.

62451309
>>62447797
Its a good idea because you actually GET something not just numbers on a screen.
He might have $1 Million to pay down later but he has atleast 4 properties providing him passive income.
Not just income to pay down the mortgages, but actually passive income on top of that.
Landlording is extremely profitable and if you don't understand it you shouldn't get into it.
But you are fucking way the fuck up by not using the VA loan ever. It's an instant ticket to real estate. Buy with the intention to landlord and you will go far.
>>62451135
>>62451198
>They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once
You don't live in reality.
There are millions of vets recieving disability. The point of disability is so the government doesnt get sued. It's workman's comp.
If what you said was true then no one would recieve it.
You probably dont even know the types of discharges that qualify you for VA disability or P&T vs TDIU. You don't know none of the details of course.
You don't care because you just want to schizopost.

This is why you dont trust stupid old people on this site. They just think they know it all and get more stubborn and post borderline schizo shit.

62451317
>>62451292
VA is for every branch.
No one is taking away VA benefits the government would be sued.
Hegseth doesnt matter.
VA benefits = Workers comp
That is the entire point of it existing.

62451419
>>62451135
>They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once.
KEK. I just realized you know even less than I thought initially.
You do know the VA doesnt give disability until after you are out of the service, right?
How can they take away your bennies when you have an honorable discharge?
You probably don't even know what types of discharges there are yet you're saying this shit.
>>62451198
Is this what happened to your dad?
Failed to switch between blindly following orders and street smarts? And died?
>>62447896
>My family quit being a military family
>because of negations of benefits
>They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once
What the fuck does this mean? Your dad was dishonorably discharged for disobeying orders? Just say that.
See this is why OP called you a schizophrenic incel.
I know Im replying way too much to these posts but this is not how reality works at all. Your view is not reality.

62454571
>>62447595
Vet here. AMA

62454901
>>62454571
Did you make a million dollars over the course of your service?

62455013
>>62451309
Getting injured in the military now is basically the only way to get the benefits you were owed. Sure it is possible to get them without any injuries. But you pretty much have to behave like a robot without a relapse moment of humanity. Extremely rare for new recruits.

62455029
>>62451309
How am I fucking way the fuck up by not taking a 6% interest mortgage on a house with 2% property taxes that appreciates significantly slower than the stock market, when I can just leave the money in index funds and watch it grow at a much higher rate?

The couple hundred dollars you pocket a month over your mortgage, HOA, fees and repairs is not going to beat leaving the principle in the stock market to compound. I'll buy a house when I'm rich.

62455051
>>62451419
Was never in but from a military family. Things went to shit after 2000. Family is too in the know on how everyone is getting screwed by the VA office. Especially their friends who had kids join. Only one dumb cousin who didn't get his benefits package. Same thing happened with most of his buddies. Exemplary service. Oops briefly questioned an order in boot camp on day five. No $35,000 package for you. He would have been better off if he shot himself in the foot a month later. No joke.

62455132
>>62447595
I'm working on getting disability now bros but I don't see it happening with the evidence I got. Looking for mental stuff. I got
>2 C&P exams with “At least as likely as not" for Depression and anxiety
>Independent medical nexus opinion for the same
>Self harm scars from time in service
>Buddy statement from someone who saw them while I was in
>Statement from someone who saw them right after I got out
I never went to behavioral health so that might just be an automatic"not service connected". I read online of people getting connected with just Buddy statements but I figure I don't have a very good chance. If I get denied and then my appeal gets denied should I get a lawyer or go through a service or something?

62455158
>>62455132
wtf where is this gif from?

62455177
>>62447595
>>62448022
What's your branch and MOS? Make sure you get everything that hurts documented. Even if you sprained your ankle and would be good with just Advil or something get it documented by the clinic. Also get a security clearance if you have the opportunity. I make $150k with it alone

62455186
>>62455158
Planet earth or another high budget nature documentary. They'd be the only ones capable of getting this magnificent(and elusive) creature on film

62455219
>>62455051
What the fuck are you talking about man, theres no such thing as a "benefits package". What $35,000 after finishing boot camp?

Just because your dad and uncles scammed disability by claiming they hurt their back after one enlistment in the 80s doesn't mean they're experts on VA disability. You sound like a retard and should do some bare-minimum googling before you speak on this subject.

62455354
>>62455013
>>62455051
You are an agitator shill. I doubt you are going on 45+ years old like you claim, since you are too retarded to live.
There is no such thing as a VA "$35,000 package".

VA is the VETERANS AFFAIRS OFFICE. You have to be in reserves with service related conditions or out of active duty.
The VA office is Workmans comp, DO YOU UNDERSTAND?
You have to actually BE INJURED or HAVE A LIFE LONG LASTING CONDITION spurred on DURING SERVICE to claim benefits.

I'm capitalizing this shit because you're too stupid to know it.
>Oops briefly questioned an order in boot camp on day five.
You can't get your discharge status changed by the VA.
What you're suggesting is that an honorable discharge can be changed by the VA to a dishonorable.
Bullshit.
Your family is lying to you and you're gullible or you are legitimately shilling in bad faith. Probably the latter given how low effort this is.

I bet I'm talking to pic rel.

62455470
>>62455354
How does one get your job? No trolling, i would actually do it.

62455496
>>62455470
are you implying he's a paid shill because he's explaining the extremely basic, google'able premise of VA disability?

62455518
>>62455496
How do i get your job, then? And what is the pay?

62455520
>>62455496
Im waiting, i dont have all day.

62455528
>>62455470
>>62455518
Juet give up, Robert. I can spot you on any board, in any thread.
Your tells are too obvious like complaining about /k/ in your first post.
And now, giving rapid short replies when caught.
It's over.

62455545
>>62455518
>>62455520
>>62455520
Sign up right here

https://gprivate.com/6ligz

62455549
>>62455520
>>62455518
>>62455470
Data center Technician ($150k). Nta

62455572
>>62455528
>>Calls me robert.
I see, it appears your technical skills are not good enough to find out my real name in short time. Does that mean the government outsources propaganda to jeets, or are they really just that bad at eglin air base?

62455580
>>62455177
Air Force 12X

62455670
>>62455572
I know Robert isn't your real name. It's the pet name that was given to you for being such a terrorist worshiping ingrate.
The name you actually like to post as is Gort Torkenson which is also the name of your twitter.
Your entire life's mission is to lie about the military.
You doordash for a living (as you posted on /k/) and spend all your money gambling in casinos on Sunday (as you posted on your twitter).

62455722
>>62455670
Fucking hilarious

62455733
>>62455670
Looks like i will be gort torkenson from now on

62455735
>>62455670
Thanks man you made my day you have no idea how funny that is

item.title: VOO and Chill — 190 replies — 213.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 190 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:06 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 144 replies — 199.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 144 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:16 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446149
>>62446088
They leave trading open so smart people can sell "for tax purposes" and get the loss on their tax return. I guess they think idiots buying 7 biĺion worthless securities for 8 cents each is funny,,,,,i do

62446238

62446242
>>62446238

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242

Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

62449186
>>62449050
all of them are short bbbyq. i guarantee it

62449344
>>62449050
>>62449186
If you are assassinated, does your short position get closed? Asking for Brian Thompson

62449448
>>62449344
Nope. His estate would get liquidated to pay off to buy back the shares.

62450370

62450461
>>62448146
The silo around eBay has become palpable

62452261
she has high res photos on her facebook. you can compare the birthmarks on her arm. the redditors are stupid as fuck it's demoralizing.

62452270
it seems that sultan didn't tweet about teddy like i thought he did. fuck's sake.

62452287
>>62452270
Its right here can you stop whining like a fatherless attention starved faggot? Youre scaring the hoes.

https://x.com/Almaadeed/status/2072817361862717939

62452325
>>62452287
ok good it displays the gamestop.com preview on the twitter page but the link he posted is to teddy.com which redirects to gamestop.com

62452826
>>62450461

62454181

62454621

62455277

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 20 replies — 53.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:37 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450909
>>62450760
In short = manipulation

62450916
>>62450902

62450937
>>62450916
Wait....what??

62450950
>thinks news drives the market

62450961
>>62450760
>just hold spot
>t. baggy
If you bought Bitcoin at the end of 2017 and held for 9 years, you would currently have no more value against gold than when you bought.

62450971
>>62450937
>not knowing who cz is

62451749
>>62450971
CZ is just another clown

62451999
>>62451749
A hardcore clown

62452810
>>62451999
the king of clown

62453044
>>62450961
I'm planning to buy silver. Is it good? Silver bar maybe

62454200
>>62450753
do you think the narrative actually influences sentiment after it's published?

62454605
>>62453044
Buy btc

62454692
>>62450950
Which fucking news?

62454719
It's over

62455393
>>62454200
What does it mean when we care the price alone

62455506
>>62450728
why not buy low cap gems
>Ansem
>Dogefi

62455701
>>62454719

item.title: Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escor… — 48 replies — 34.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escor…

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-07-07 14:37 UTC

OP

Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escorts.

Surely there's other rich anons here that have played out that fantasy? How was it? Anything you'd do differently if you had to do it again?

This is financial advice since although I won't miss that amount of money on a once-in-a-lifetime night I still want to get my money's worth.

Early replies (first 3)

62450611
Nigga thinks he's in a porn video lmao

62450618
>>62450597
Do you live in brazil or something? Three "high class" escorts in the US would rape you for 4k a piece

62450623
>>62450611
Yeah pretty much lol but keep in mind I'm 40 so I've had my time to think about what it is I really want in life before my time is up and this is one of those. Like, I know it's not going to be like in a porn video I'm not naive but I'm hoping to at least experience some of the acts only possible with three other women.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451490
>>62451420
>Maybe start out with a threesome?
It's a one-and-done for me so I'd rather just go all-out and like I said before I'm already sure I'm physically capable.

62451497
>>62451458
>Maybe that's just what being past 30 feels like.
Off-topic but there was a period of about a year where I didn't work out too much and lost physical fitness I'll never get back. But in your case I'm sure if you run and hit the gym you'll feel much MUCH younger again. I still mog the vast majority of people in their 20s fitness-wise and like I said I just turned 40.

62451571
>>62451329
you should never have to lube for sex anon..

62451780
Just do it, ravage those whores like you would in your dreams, thats what's "making it" is all about right?

62451787
>>62451780
>making it
>paying for sex

wew

62451846
>>62451484
I was approached by the pair of women while at a club on both occasions.

62452092
>>62451787
Why do you think there's a drought of luxury escorts everywhere else in the world during the annual WEF? It's because all the top escorts go there because all the most powerful men on the planet pay for pussy. You think they waste time on tinder? No, they pay for prime pussy.

Time is money, and you're wasting a lot of time trying to fuck mediocre pussy

62452170
>>62450816
>I've done enough "one-by-one" in my life that I'm specifically not interested in that
>>62450852
>One thing I'm worried about is if the women aren't interested... Since I'll be paying them what if they're just like dead fish or worse

If you want to do it right you need to dip in the scene in your area with some one-on-ones to figure out the optimal combination of girls. Girls all have certain girls they play well with and enjoy working with. If you're just going in cold with 3 random whores who have never met each other you're heavily stacking the odds against yourself having a good time. I've done a few threesomes with paid girls and the best times were always with their SW BFFs, who of course I only choose if they are also hot.

62452201
>>62450623
They're prostitutes so have them do the dirtyest shit. Get your ass eatin the entire time. I personally would start off with a classy 3way footjob

62452205
>>62452092
In my mid 20s living in Boston I was able to pull a new girl off tinder almost every day with minimal effort. I had a rotation going for a while. These are 18-22 WASP college chicks attending elite universities with minimal or sometimes zero body counts. They're there because they want to fuck you. There's nothing more validating. No lube or condoms required. That is the definition of prime pussy.

paying for luxury escorts is a cope for men who never could or can no longer experience this

62452461
>>62452205
You're in your mid-20s. Almost nobody has made it financially at your age. Come back when you're 35 and see how that shit goes for you.

Of course pulling young women is easy when you're young yourself. When you're pushing 40, you'll have to put in an insane amount of effort to bag top tier women in their early 20s, and the free juice is simply not worth the squeeze

62452542
>>62450597
Holy shit my man you will have such buyer's remorse after that. Please don't.

62452868
>>62451571
In an ideal world that would be the case!

>>62451780
I mean, I have the means to do so now but like I said I'm also 40 so now it's more of a bucket list thing and no longer a thing I need to do to prove something (to myself) It's a curiosity if you will but a curiosity that won't be satisfied if they aren't into it at all...

62452881
>>62451846
Ah. Yeah that would be a lot different than reaching out to independents

>>62452170
For obvious reasons this will need to be discreet so can't exactly go to any meetups (I've made a good life for myself let's leave it at that)
>Girls all have certain girls they play well with and enjoy working with
Yeah but I know that's mostly out of my control. Besides, I'd rather they DO know each other well beforehand.

62452887
>>62452170
>'ve done a few threesomes with paid girls and the best times were always with their SW BFFs, who of course I only choose if they are also hot.
Hey, great to hear, fren. That's exactly my plan.

>>62452201
Lmao. I'm (unfortunately?) pretty vanilla but hope you can make it so you can live out that crazy fantasy fren.

>>62452205
>paying for luxury escorts is a cope for men who never could or can no longer experience this
I mean I doubt you could pull THREE hot girls together in one night at the same time. Like, I grew up with 6'2 athletes and even the most they would do is a threesome (which they said always sucked because one girl was always a jealous bitch) or an orgy.

62452919
OP you do you, but considering you want STD tests (which can be easily forged, and if you think they can't you're a fucking moron) if you were to catch an TSD, I think you would spiral as you would have paid an exorbitant amount for an STD and an experience that I think won't live up to your expectations. The other anons are right, these women have no synergy if they don't know eachother. You do you though, let us know how it goes.

62452964
>>62452919
Yeah the STD thing does worry me especially since like I said I've a life I want to maintain afterwards
>an experience that I think won't live up to your expectations.
That's fair and I'm sure you're right but it's mostly about curiosity. For instance, my philosophy is you only have one life and obviously you can't do everything but you do need to think about what's worth doing before you pass away for YOU specifically. I'm still fit especially for my age so for me this fantasy of pleasing multiple women at once has persisted

>>62452919
>The other anons are right, these women have no synergy if they don't know eachother
Noted and appreciate the advice. Truly. Will definitely have to look more into std test forgery hmm...

62454057
>>62451490
No no my good sir, once you experience multiple girls there is no simply "one and done"

62454064
>>62450597

Do it, record it, sell it ... make others PAY for the privilege of watching you fuck hot whores

62454078
>>62450597
Shit I want to try if you want inspo

Two woman tittyfuck so find two of the girls at least some C cups, make a human triangle with them. Do one girl does dick, one does balls, one does anal. However if you can't convince her at least see if they would do two licking cock at same time and one handling balls full time. Also do the triple suck if you have size like one does hit two lick shaft and definitely get them to compete licking the head before you bust.

Do the stack you do doggie on one big titty girl facing you the other twerks over ones face.

If you can get them to dyke out for your pleasure get them to do the trib pussy licking combo maybe watch that awhile then just treat them as a buffet grab breasts, kiss one while she gets eaten out.

OK well I just came typing this so hope it helps. :)

OH almost forgot if you can get them to get tired up and hit one with a Hitachi and get her off is another kinky thing.

62454089
>>62450597
>I still want to get my money's worth.
you're still in poverty mindset then.

if you've actually made it you don't care about $6k and would be looking for things to blow money on.

While I've never blown money on that many women at once, I have had sex with 4 women in the same evening and that was fun. I just did them one at a time. However since one was my wife, one was my ex-wife, one was my mistress, and the other was a good friend I liked, it wasn't the same as having 4 strangers. I'm not sure I'd like that. I prefer women that know what I like.

62454102
With escort you can’t see genuine connection, genuine people, genuine chemistry, genuine memory, genuine heart, genuine relationships, genuine love, genuine happiness, genuine vibes, genuine mind, genuine gender, genuine honest, genuine commitments, genuine vegan

62454120
This would only work with actual pornstars that have worked together before, not random prostitutes

62454327
>>62454102
It's just sex. People aren't trying to marry the escorts, lmao

62454984
>>62454057
This is a worry for sure but I've actually done lots of "one and done" stuff in my life. Short of crack and heroin if it's a drug I've done it. If it's a sex fetish (aside from shit puke and sounding) I've tried it. Perhaps that's why like I said it's mostly a curiosity for me. But noted. Thanks for your input.

>>62454078
>Two woman tittyfuck so find two of the girls at least some C cups, make a human triangle with them. Do one girl does dick, one does balls. However if you can't convince her at least see if they would do two licking cock at same time and one handling balls full time
Definitely going to do that! Thanks!

>>62454078
>Also do the triple suck if you have size like one does hit two lick shaft and definitely get them to
Hmm can definitely try that.

>compete licking the head before you bust.
Yeah the only thing that sucks is they have strict "finishing" policies. I'm guessing for STD prevention. I think most I'll be able to get is a boob/bodyshot

>Do the stack you do doggie on one big titty girl facing you the other twerks over ones face.
Definitely!

>If you can get them to dyke out for your pleasure
Yes! Prerequisite for sure! There's so much more that gets unlocked if they are all into pleasuring each other.

>>62454078
>OK well I just came typing this so hope it helps.
For sure fren I feel like we're on the same page here in why it's a fun curiosity!

>hit one with a Hitachi
Forgot about that! Will definitely have to bring up sex toy stuff as prerequisite as well! Cheers!

62455010
>>62454089
>if you've actually made it you don't care about $6k
Off-topic but I've met CEOs worth 8 figures in my life. They were the cheapest stingiest human beings you can imagine. Would tip at most 10% on a meal and would argue if the steak was medium-rare instead of medium (and then only eat a few bites anyway)

>I just did them one at a time. However since one was my wife, one was my ex-wife, one was my mistress, and the other was a good friend I liked, it wasn't the same as having 4 strangers
Yeah like that's exactly what I DON'T want. So prerequisite is the escorts need to be into each other!

62455032
>>62454102
>With escort you can’t see genuine connection, genuine people
Meh. I've had enough of that. When you get older you'll also realize nothing is as genuine as you think. For instance if a friend moves to another continent you drift apart. Why is that? And the most popular people are literal caricatures. Why is that I wonder? Similarly there's a boom in pets. Hmm, I wonder why that is.

>>62454120
Hopefully not but something to think about... Cheers.

62455181
>>62450597
if youre gonna spend that much you might as well hire a high class anesthesiologist to administer some drugs too

62455245
>>62455181
See my other post. Already got my pharma game down. Good point though for others to consider. Pharma sex = better sex.

62455270
>>62455245
Out of curiosity, what's your pharma game? Amphetamines, SSRIs and the classic -afil PDE5 inhibitor?

item.title: AI is dying — 30 replies — 30.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-07-07 14:59 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449981
>>62449963
I'm happy for you, or sorry that happened to you.

62449983
>>62449963
>believing corporate media slop intended to make poorfags stay out

62450012
>>62449983
>they don't want us to make money

62450055
>>62449963
>single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

It is, but there is so little "100x" human and definitely no one is working for that specific company.

62450135
>>62450012
Speaking for myself, I do not like the idea of poors entering my country club.

62450162
>>62449963
It isn't dying, but people are waking up to the fact that it's not "Jarvis, run my company" situation and that there is a hard skill curve to coordinating agent pipelines.

62450194
>>62450012
True, but the game is already rigged for the casino either way.

62450337
Not reading all that but AI isn't going anywhere, the pace may slow down but it's not going to be binned off.

62450351
CEOniggers once again proven to be 40 IQ retards.

62450414
>>62449963
Our CEO is going all-in on AI for software development and it has boosted our throughput by 2-3x. It's not slop code, either.
We have unlimited tokens.
t. SWE at startup

62450423
>>62449963
Over the weekend my ceo told me we are adopting more ai

62452074
>>62450414
>It's not slop code
lol. LMAO even

62452087
>inc.com
What
>a tech ceo
Which company

62452090
>>62450012
>rich people investing 3 trillion into AI the last 4 years
>Nvidia a 5 trillion dollar company
>the AI market is over now, because it's over
Makes you think.

62452160
>>62450135
Exactly. There is the door so would you kindly leave?

62452209
Kek my company banned it years ago when some high level executive fed sensitive financial data to chat gpt. It qas back when chat gpt was new and novel and he was probably a boomer that didn't know what chatbots are and thought it was a super intelligence.

62452880
AI is a technology and it won't go way, it will evolve into different tools that will enhance the production. The problem is that corporations are using as an excuse to control the resources and make big data centers to get more info from you and sell it.

62452896
>>62449963
This article is a month old, fucktard.

62452909
>>62452896
Precisely, baggie.

62452925
>>62452909
continuation pattern

62452935
>>62452925
lmao

62452942
>>62452935
>you: heh my pattern is bearish
>"i disagree, I think it's bullish"
>REEEEEEEE TA SUCKS FUCK YOU

62453140
>>62452942
I never implied that. I implied you're delusional if you believe exponential growth is any sustainable long-term.

62453190
>>62453140
Probably because you are retarded.

62454009
>>62452074
All the code is written and reviewed by hand. We have custom AI agents for repetitive tasks and custom tool calls error-checking.
Works great, amazing ROI

62454042
>>62449963

There is no way I am going to ever write a regular expression or a complex SQL statement without AI EVER AGAIN.
AI solution saves time and is easy to verify.

62455423
>>62449963
its joever

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 13 replies — 21.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:24 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62448322
>>62448316
Just bullshit. At 4% annually your link would need to be worth $9125 per token.

62448378
>>62448316
He is just talking bullshit. They just pushed all their users to pay through stripe. There is not really a pathway to imagine corporate successes translating into token holder value. Payment abstraction layer and staking were both a flop and they were what link holders always said would be the way they would make it but now instead they deleted the past marketing materials, disallowed comment on it in their AMA and pushed Stripe payments. It has unironically never been more obvious and is so obvious it is borderline in the exit phase

62448411
>>62448322
10k Link eoy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448505
I wish.

62448514
>>62448322
>>62448378
Kek why do fuddies always flag themselves as newfags by replying to pasta. Retarded fuddies. Just bought more LINK, seethe and dilate.

>posted from my iPhone from my beach house in Mexico

62448520
>>62448514
Not fudding - I bought another 1000 this week. What's your vision for where this is going?

62448521
>>62448316
if you follow the money there's a good reward waiting for those who stake

62450512
bump

62451573
>>62448520
I'm a cuckold and I just LOVE holding Chainlink. Whenever Sergey dumps millions of tokens on Binance I get super hard! Fuddies can cope and seethe but this is my fetish and you can't kink shame me

62451657
he didnt pay his ladyboy

62452033
>>62448514
Been here longer than you. I bought link in 2018 with early biz and sold when it pumped and it was clearly not building out for token holders alongside literally all of early biz before the necrotic, tech illiterate, petrified fossil of late normie greed cultists flooded in to fill the cavity and be the findom submissive of Sir Gay who sells to them with zero prospect of value return for token holders, even selling node operator rewards with the network unable to support 30% of function through revenues. You are not a smart investor, you literally just get hyped about cheap "announcements" which mean nothing for token holders whatsoever and are literally written to seem like they do with zero actual technical reality so that they can use them to sell tokens to you to fundraise for the company and salaries. Your analysis amounts to "price went up here before and I missed it but I won't miss THIS time because it will TOTALLY happen again, just..cuz!! Oh, did you see the news on x institution using CLL for price feeds?! I am going to be SO rich! No, I don't understand how this will impact actual token demand or how the token will fit into the equation at all, but the announcement is from Chainlink Labs, so it must somehow!!!"

62454188
>>62451573
The funny thing is this is LITERALLY the link holder psychology.

62455614
>>62448316
yes many rupees await you if you buy the Chainlink token sir

item.content:encoded:
WTFWT

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:59 UTC

OP

W T F W T
T
F
W
T

Early replies (first 3)

62449036
>>62448983
Short liquidations for a while now. You cannot just let it fall every single day, so the goyim will short it down to the ground all night and win!

62451818
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62454618
>>62448983
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455828
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

item.title: Let's assume crypto is dead. — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Let's assume crypto is dead.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:51 UTC

OP

Or atleast life changing gains can no longer be made.
What is next?
I am thinking about yoloing into ai startups. I am going back to the workforce after 2 years of semi-neeting and I MUST have the same high as when I traded shitcoins or my life will be miserable.
$100000 is unironically life changing money where I live.

Early replies (first 3)

62455773
>>62455755
We need to find oversold stocks that are too small to appear on wallstreetbets, Load up on calls, then pump it until it hits those retards

62455794
>>62455773
Are you implying a pump and dump scheme? You know do know that this shit does not work like crypto and it is highly illegal, right?

62455795
>>62455755

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: /biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! — 14 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X!

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-07 14:43 UTC

OP

/biz/ 8 years ago
>BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! YOU JUST NEED 1K DOLLARS
/biz/ 5 years ago
>OMG BRO SOMEONE THREW 3500 BUCKS/10 ETH INTO THIS COIN, WE HAVE WHALES! I'M UP 400K TODAY!
/biz/ now
>You should NEVER do calls, options, invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case, and if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid because it will crash no matter what. Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.

Where did these people come from? The board is fucking dead but did we get an influx of unironic retired boomers giving out advice that 'worked' in 1966 and is now totally fucking worthless?

Early replies (first 3)

62453596
>>62453585
we've always had 3 or 4 boomers here. But now everyone else left.

coincidentally all those other "people" left when they made the capcha harder and made ban evasion more difficult.

62453601
>>62453585
Jannies killed the board. You cant share alpha or gambles or anything here now.

62453607
cant share any cryptos that arent in the top 100 coins lmao. the 2024/2025 janny (if theyre still here) turned this place into bag holder general

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453619
>You should NEVER do calls, options

True, why do broke retards think they can beat rich smart people

62453628
The start a business fags are just trolling usually from what I see, provide no legit advice.
Invest in VOO fags should be banned just for being so boring. Why even bother to post this. Yes everyone knows put all your money in sp500 for 50 years you will make a few millions. Discussion is for how to beat this.

62453703
>>62453585
Its because making it in crypto is over, the newfag business retards and stock retards took over.
>>62453628
This, people here are retarded and think buying a broad stock index fund is some amazing genius plan but its the bare minimum to seperate yourself from a NPC.

62453842
Everything you posted was nonsense except the part about VOO

62453878
>>62453585
Circumstances have changed, I’m unironically smiling at my VOO and AIQ gains yet back then I was buying options and cryptos. All that money is gone now. I’m 30 now I need to think about my future seriously.
>>62453842
Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.

62453881
>>62453585
Its a glow psyop now.

62454979
>>62453585
maybe people learned not to waste money snd time on dangerous "investments"

62455028
>>62453878
>Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.
Like a billionaire who insider traded the market and lived in luxury until he died at almost 100 told you to?

62455042
>>62453601
>>62453607
This.
Try posting something that isnt a normie popular investment and it gets nuked instantly.

62455297
Nice thumbnail bro.

62455311
>>62453585
/biz/ now is more
>Start a power washing business and buy tech stocks
